summary: Summary: Early in the call, Speaker1, frustrated and sad, offers to help Speaker2 move in with Speaker2’s mother to protect the family as deployment approaches. Speaker2 responds with fear, anger, and sadness, resisting the move and urging Speaker1 to stay behind to care for the children. Mid-conversation Speaker2 is surprised by a three-week deployment timeline, which heightens tension and desperation. Speaker1, fearful about Iraq and frustrated by the prospect of at least six months away, expresses sadness and says "I love you." As emotions intensify for both speakers—sadness, frustration, and moments of surprise—they nevertheless reaffirm commitment to one another and, ending on a note of tentative happiness and resolve, promise to make the situation work despite the separation.
